producer_note: Founded by Jayson Woodbridge; Australian project 'Ancient Way' launched 2004 producing single-vineyard Shiraz from Ancient Way Vineyard in the Greenock area of Barossa Valley.
Jerome Coessens took over the family estate in 2003; all wines come from a single 5.5-acre Pinot Noir parcel ('Largillier') in Ville-sur-Arce, Cote des Bar (Aube). Certified organic since 2021.
Founded in 2005 by Robert Mondavi with children Tim and Marcia; estate located on Pritchard Hill in the Vaca mountains of Napa Valley. Produces a single Cabernet-based proprietary red.
Founded 1985 in Marlborough's Wairau Valley; LVMH-owned since 2003. Sources from Wairau Valley sub-regions (Rapaura, Renwick, Brancott Valley) plus Central Otago for Pinot Noir.
Founded 1996 by Claude and Katherine Blankiet on 46 acres above Yountville in the Mayacamas foothills. Paradise Hills Vineyard produces Cabernet, Merlot and proprietary red blends.
Founded 2008 by oenologist Mario Ercolino and Roberta Pirone in Paternopoli, Irpinia. ~15 ha on volcanic soils at 450-550m producing Aglianico, Fiano, Greco and Falanghina.
Founded 2010 by Grupo Penaflor with winemaker Rodolfo Sadler. Sources from two terroirs: Cruz de Piedra in Maipu (97 ha) and Uco Valley vineyards. Argentine Producer of the Year IWSC 2014.
Founded 1890 by Eugenio Bustos; oldest winery in Uco Valley and first to plant Malbec there. 380 ha across Paraje Altamira, La Consulta and Eugenio Bustos. Owned by VSPT since 1999.
Founded 1910 in Celles-sur-Ource (Aube); 4th-generation estate led by Aurelien Gerbais farming ~18 ha of Kimmeridgian limestone in Cote des Bar. Sustainable certified.
Founded 1987 by Aurelio Montes and Douglas Murray. Headquartered at Apalta (Colchagua) but sources from Aconcagua, Casablanca, Curico, Itata, Maule, Leyda, Marchigue and Zapallar.
Founded 1983 by Hideyuki Miyakawa and three families on the Tuscan coast; sole owners since 1999. Organic from inception, producing Suvereto DOCG reds plus Vermentino and Sangiovese.
Founded 1972 by Alejandro Fernandez in Pesquera de Duero; pioneer of Ribera del Duero DO. 200 ha of Tempranillo on limestone-clay soils, family-owned, now led by Fernandez's granddaughters.
Sixth-generation family winery founded 1823 in Magre (Alto Adige). Manages 85 ha across many sub-zones with 30 grape varieties; fully Demeter biodynamic/organic since 2024.
Founded 1875 in Santiago by Luis Pereira Cotapos. One of Chile's oldest wineries, sourcing from Maule, Maipo, Colchagua, Cachapoal and Casablanca.
Launched 2001 by Jean-Philippe and Jean-Guillaume Bret as a Maconnais negociant alongside their family estate La Soufrandiere in Pouilly-Vinzelles. Certified organic since 2006.
Volcanic-soil-focused project started 2008 by Undurraga. Sources from plots with volcanic influence in Maipo, Leyda, Rapel and Cauquenes. Sustainable certified.
Carrille family estate; Jean-Marie purchased in 1967, Philippe took over 1989. 33 ha (97% Merlot) on south-facing clay-limestone slopes of Castillon. Long-time certified organic.
Cooperative founded 1893 in Terlano (Alto Adige); 143 grower-members tending 190 ha. Specialises in DOC Alto Adige Terlano whites (Pinot Bianco, Chardonnay, Sauvignon).
Family-run Bruwer estate in Robertson Valley, in the family since 1898. 9th-generation Huguenot descendants; natural-yeast 'Methode Ancienne' winemaking with Sauvignon Blanc and Chardonnay flagships.
Founded 1950s by Livio Felluga in Brazzano. ~160 ha across Colli Orientali del Friuli and Collio. Famous for Terre Alte white blend; family-run, now led by son Andrea Felluga.
First vines planted 1977 by Dr Lagan; pioneer of Margaret River. Acquired 2005 by the Rathbone family. Known for Cabernet Sauvignon, Chardonnay and Semillon Sauvignon Blanc blends.
Founded 1991 by Stewart Elms; first vintage 1997. Four estate vineyards on north-facing loess slopes in Bannockburn, fully Demeter biodynamic certified 2010. Renowned for Pinot Noir.
Founded 2015 in Gaiole in Chianti at 540m elevation. Three vineyard sites (Bertinga, Adine, Vertine) producing Chianti Classico from Sangiovese and Merlot. Gambero Rosso Best Emergent Producer 2022.
Founded early 1980s by Robert O'Callaghan in Tanunda; traditional winemaking with old basket presses and open fermenters. Famous for Basket Press Shiraz from old, low-yielding Barossa vines.
Family estate in Wettolsheim run by the Barthelme brothers, biodynamic since 1997. 25 ha across six Grand Cru sites: Schlossberg, Hengst, Furstentum, Steingrubler, Pfersigberg, Wineck-Schlossberg.
Founded 1950 by Jean-Louis Vergnon; 5+ ha Grand Cru estate in Le Mesnil-sur-Oger, Cote des Blancs. 100% Chardonnay Blanc de Blancs, no chaptalisation/no malo, low or zero dosage.
Founded early 1990s by oenologist Salvo Foti, named after the 1435 Etna winegrowers' guild. Based in Milo (East Etna); ~30 ha on volcanic soils up to 1,200m. Natural, indigenous-yeast winemaking.
Family estate in Verzenay (Grand Cru) since the late 18th century; current Rousseaux-Batteux brand created 1976. Now led by 4th-generation Adrien Rousseaux, focused on Pinot Noir from Verzenay.
Founded 1870 by Don Maximiano Errazuriz. Vineyards across warm Aconcagua Andes plus the cooler Aconcagua Costa estate near the Pacific. Flagship Don Maximiano Cabernet blend.
5th-generation grower; 6.3 ha across 30 parcels in Le Breuil and Festigny (Vallee de la Marne). Predominantly Pinot Meunier; biodynamic with homeopathy/lunar approach.
Founded early 1990s by Jean-Paul Valette (former owner of Chateau Pavie); first vines planted 1993. 90 ha at 700-900m in Pirque (Alto Maipo); flagship is 100% Cabernet Sauvignon since 2019.
Founded 1877 by cousins Ilario and Leopoldo Ruffino in Pontassieve. Seven Tuscan estates spanning Chianti Classico, Montalcino and Bolgheri. Famous for Riserva Ducale; produces 33M bottles annually.
Founded 1922 in Echevronne (between Cote de Beaune and Cote de Nuits). 32 ha certified organic (Ecocert 2011) producing wines from Gevrey-Chambertin to Chassagne-Montrachet and Givry 1er Cru.
Tobias Vineyards is the project of Toby and Dawn Pauli Forman, hand-farming 16 acres of Cabernet Sauvignon on Howell Mountain in Napa Valley. Heritage tied to Ric Forman and Bill Pauli families.
Founded 1835 in Jerez de la Frontera by Manuel Maria Gonzalez Angel; partnered with Robert Blake Byass. ~650 ha mostly in Jerez Superior. Maker of Tio Pepe Fino, world's #1 Fino sherry.
Founded 1876 in St. Helena by German brothers Jacob and Frederick Beringer; oldest continuously operating Napa winery (made sacramental wine through Prohibition). Sources widely across Napa AVAs.
Yering Station, established 1838, is Victoria's first vineyard. Rathbone family-owned since 1996. Sources from five Yarra Valley vineyards in Yarra Glen and Coldstream.
